name: db_schema_analyzer
description: Analyze a PostgreSQL database table schema in one call: shows columns (name, type, nullable, default), constraints (PK, FK, unique, check), and indexes with definitions, plus sample data.
when_to_use: Use when you need to understand a database table's structure. Replaces 3-4 separate psql commands (one for columns, one for constraints, one for indexes, one for sample data) with a single script call.

parameters.name: table_name
parameters.description: Name of the database table to analyze

examples.expected: Columns, constraints, indexes, and sample data for the 'contact' table.
Full schema analysis for the 'users' table.

cost_saving_rationale: Combines 4 SQL queries (columns info, constraints, indexes, sample data) into one script call, reducing 3+ round trips. Also keeps output organized and eliminates parsing overhead.
